stories.title: Isa Torres Enters the Portal — The Year's Best Player Is on the Move

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
🚨 The No. 1 player in college softball just entered the portal.

Isa Torres — 2026 D1 Player of the Year, ACC POY — is leaving FSU.

.530 BA | .978 SLG | .591 OBP | 78 runs

Texas and Texas A&M already linked. One year of eligibility.

#CollegeSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Isa Torres just posted arguably the greatest offensive season in NCAA softball history before entering the portal:

.530 batting average
.978 slugging percentage
.591 on-base percentage
78 runs scored

All FSU records. She has one year left.

#CollegeSoftball #SoftballPortal

article.headline: 2026 D1 Player of the Year Isa Torres Enters Transfer Portal — Texas Ties Fuel Recruiting Race

research.facts:
Isa Torres — 2026 D1 Softball Player of the Year Enters Portal
**Sources:**
- 247Sports: "Florida State Seminoles softball standout Isa Torres is entering the NCAA Transfer Portal"
- WCTV: "FSU Softball star Isa Torres to enter transfer portal"
- Fox Sports: "Florida State softball star Isa Torres announces she's entering transfer portal"
- Washington Post: "Florida State softball star Isa Torres announces she's entering transfer portal"
- On3: "FSU star Isa Torres, the ACC Player of the Year, to enter transfer portal"
- Tomahawk Nation: "FSU loses ACC Player of the Year, D1 Softball Player of the Year Isa Torres to transfer portal"
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence — multiple sources):**
- Isa Torres entered the NCAA Transfer Portal June 8, 2026
- Named 2026 D1 Softball Player of the Year (multiple sources)
- Named 2026 ACC Player of the Year (multiple sources)
- Two-time First Team All-American
- Set FSU records in: batting average (.530), runs scored (78), slugging percentage (.978), on-base percentage (.591)
- Georgetown, Texas native — hometown connection to Texas
- One year of eligibility remaining
- Texas and Texas A&M reported as early linked destinations
**Freshness:** Announced June 8, 2026 — Day 1 of portal window. Coverage today (June 9) focuses on recruiting implications and destination speculation.

stories.title: Portal Day 2 Tracker — Polar, Rijo, and More Join the Wave

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Softball portal Day 2 update:

→ Moriah Polar (Purdue) — All-American OF, 218 career hits, 103 RBI. In the portal.
→ Atalyia Rijo (Arkansas) — .342 BA, 7 HR, 42 RBI. In the portal.

Beachum, Edwards, Swan still uncommitted.

This class is stacked. 🥎 #SoftballPortal

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Still waiting on Beachum and Edwards to commit. Two of the best players in college softball are available right now.

Beachum (FSU): .417 BA, 16 HR, 66 RBI, 1 yr eligibility
Edwards (LSU): .802 SLG, 18 HR, 2 yrs remaining

Where are they going? 🥎 #SoftballPortal

research.facts:
Portal Day 2 Tracker — Polar, Rijo Join the Wave
**Sources:**
- Sports Illustrated/OnSI: "Purdue Softball All-American Makes Surprising Transfer Portal Move"
- Whole Hog Sports: "Report: Arkansas softball shortstop Atalyia Rijo enters transfer portal"
- On3 Transfer Portal Wire: ongoing tracker
- D1Softball Transfer Tracker: ongoing tracker
- MSN: "College softball transfer portal heats up with top talent moves"
- SI: "2026 College Softball Transfer Portal Rankings: Top 10 Available Players"
- SI: "These are the Hottest Names in the College Softball Transfer Portal"
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Moriah Polar (Purdue OF) entered portal — announced June 8, 2026
- All-American career at Purdue
- 218 career hits, 103 RBI, 137 runs scored across three seasons
- One year of eligibility remaining
- Atalyia Rijo (Arkansas SS) entered portal — reported June 8, 2026
- .342 batting average in 2026 (career high)
- 13 doubles, 7 home runs, 42 RBI (career highs)
- Day 1 entries still uncommitted as of June 9: Beachum (FSU), Edwards (LSU), Swan (ASU)
- Beachum: .417 BA, 16 HR, 66 RBI, 1 yr eligibility (FSU)
- Edwards: .802 SLG, 18 HR, 2 yrs eligibility (LSU)
- Swan: .383 BA, 12 HR, 55 RBI (ASU)
- Portal window: June 8–22, 2026 (15 days)
**Freshness:** Active portal window, Day 2. New entries continuing.

stories.title: Texas Builds for a Three-Peat — Kavan and Stewart Lead the Way Back

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Texas returns Teagan Kavan AND Katie Stewart as seniors for 2027.

The two-time MOP and the 4x WCWS-HR hitter are both coming back for one more shot at a three-peat.

The Longhorns have 11 days to complete their portal class. 🤘

#Softball #LHN

- 11 days remain in portal window (as of June 9)
**Freshness:** Three-peat angle is new framing for ongoing recruitment window story.

stories.title: Alabama Portal Tracker — Murphy Rebuilds the Offense Around Briski

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Alabama lost 7 seniors from its 49-7 season.

Briski is back — the offense needs rebuilt.

Murphy has 11 portal days to find the pieces. With the ace locked in, every offensive transfer can see what they're walking into.

The Tide hunts. #Alabama #CollegeSoftball

Alabama Portal Tracker

- AOL: Alabama softball transfer portal tracker
- The Crimson White: "Recapping Alabama softball's recent roster movement"
- SI/Alabama: "Alabama Softball 2026 Offseason Roster Tracker"
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Alabama went 49-7 in 2026, No. 1 overall seed
- Staff posted 1.60 ERA, 22 shutouts
- Seven seniors graduated: Abby Duchscherer, Marlie Giles, Alea Johnson, Kinley Pate, Larissa Preuitt, Alexis Pupillo, Kristen White
- Jocelyn Briski (SEC Pitcher of the Year) confirmed returning for 2027 (from June 8 research)
- Patrick Murphy is head coach (hired after June 7, 2026 coaching change context)
**Note:** June 8 pipeline covered Briski return angle; June 9 angle is Alabama portal hunting for offensive pieces.

stories.title: Oklahoma Begins Its Reload — Gasso's Most Important Offseason in Years

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Oklahoma missed the WCWS for the first time since 2015 in 2026.

Now Patty Gasso rebuilds without Kendall Wells and several key seniors.

Eight national titles. She's done this before.

Can OU return to Devon Park in 2027? #Softball #Sooners

article.headline: Oklahoma's Reset: Patty Gasso Has Rebuilt Before — The Question Is How Fast

research.facts:
Oklahoma Rebuild — Gasso's Reload
**Sources:**
- SI/OnSI: "OU softball transfer portal tracker: Let the chaos begin"
- On3: "2026-27 Softball Transfer Portal"
- General context from pipeline story history
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Oklahoma did not reach WCWS in 2026 (eliminated by Mississippi State in Super Regionals)
- First missed WCWS since 2015
- Kendall Wells had 39 HRs in 2026 (one shy of Megan Grant's record)
- Patty Gasso has eight national championships at Oklahoma
- OU historically reloads quickly through recruiting and portal
**Confidence:** MEDIUM on Oklahoma-specific portal actions (no specific names confirmed yet). Story angle is program narrative/outlook rather than specific transactions.
